title: Align domain-scale fixtures and CI with production contracts
goal: Harden the merged PR #1442 benchmark so identifiers, timestamps, cursor workloads and CI assertions remain deterministic while representing production contracts and preserving fail-closed fixture validation.

acceptance.assertion: Node and edge fixtures use deterministic RFC 4122 UUID-v4-shaped identifiers, and cursor thresholds exercise the lexicographic distribution of production-shaped UUID keys rather than sequential prefixed text keys.
acceptance.verifier: unit tests plus exact fixture and workload receipts

acceptance.assertion: created_at increases monotonically in insertion order across at least the scale_1m edge count and does not wrap after 28 days.
acceptance.verifier: bounded regression test over timestamp boundary cases

acceptance.assertion: The domain-scale workflow derives expected loaded row counts from the generated manifest and contains no duplicated CI-profile count constants.
acceptance.verifier: workflow inspection and successful exact-head GitHub Actions run

acceptance.assertion: A fixture whose CSV and colocated manifest hash are changed consistently but whose declared row count is false still fails validation; any performance fast path requires an independently trusted binding and measured benefit.
acceptance.verifier: existing self-consistent row-count-lie regression test plus performance evidence for any fast path

acceptance.assertion: The benchmark either safely accepts native Windows absolute psql paths with focused tests or explicitly documents and tests a POSIX-only execution contract; unsafe psql meta-command characters remain rejected.
acceptance.verifier: path-literal regression tests and blueprint contract

acceptance.assertion: Budget failures continue to write the machine-readable report before returning a nonzero exit status.

metadata.current_test_receipt: python3 -B scripts/tests/test_domain_scale.py: 12 tests pass at merge commit
metadata.current_validation_receipt: python3 -B scripts/performance/domain_scale.py validate: status pass at merge commit
confirmed_findings: sequential non-UUID fixture identifiers diverge from node and edge contracts and production UUID-v4 creation
day-first timestamp generation destroys insertion-order time correlation
workflow count constants duplicate the generated manifest
rejected_findings: skip CSV structural and row-count validation solely after matching a digest stored in the same mutable manifest
already_resolved_findings: write failure reports before returning nonzero from check
metadata.safety_boundary: Do not start implementation until the active Weltgewebe PR #1440 repo-wide lease is released or an exact nonconflict proof becomes available.
does_not_establish: a need to change production schemas
a need to support native Windows
permission to merge without a current downloadable full diff and exact-head self-review
